professional_persona: Aravind Biradar, a veteran toddy tapper and community organizer, blends deep sap‑extraction expertise with creative storytelling, and they use improvised ‘jugaad’ solutions to run a modest cooperative and mentor younger villagers.
linguistic_persona: Aravind Biradar, a fluent Kannada speaker who navigates daily life with functional Hindi and conversational English, they pick up new words through folk songs and storytelling despite being unable to read or write in any script.
sports_persona: Aravind Biradar, an enthusiastic cricket player who joins weekend matches on the village field, they cheer for the state team in the IPL and lead friendly rivalries with neighboring hamlets, using their competitive spirit to energize the squad.
arts_persona: Aravind Biradar, a passionate folk‑art aficionado who drums the chende during Yakshagana evenings, they weave epic tales reminiscent of legendary storyteller Kadu Siddappa, and sketch palm‑grove landscapes inspired by the works of artist B. V. Karanth.
travel_persona: Aravind Biradar, who prefers short trips to nearby hills and forest trails for plant scouting, they occasionally travel to the historic city of Mysore for festivals, and dream of a pilgrimage to Varanasi once their children can contribute financially.
culinary_persona: Aravind Biradar, who relishes traditional palm‑sap fermented drinks and savours dishes like bisi bele bath, neer dosa with coconut chutney, and spicy mango pickle, they often experiment with toddy infusion to create new flavors for village gatherings.
persona: Aravind Biradar is a 46‑year‑old imaginative toddy tapper who weaves folk stories, leads a community cooperative with a competitive edge, and habitually forgets appointments while dreaming of his children’s education.
cultural_background: Aravind hails from the rural heart of Shimoga in Karnataka, where his family has been engaged in toddy tapping for generations. His everyday life is woven with Kannada folk traditions – from the thunderous drums of Yakshagana to the colorful rituals of Ugadi and Dasara. The village’s palm groves, modest homes, and the local temple form the backdrop of his world, where practical know‑how of tapping meets a vivid imagination, often sparking a bit of ‘jugaad’ to make life easier.
linguistic_background: Aravind’s mother tongue is Kannada, which he speaks fluently and uses for all daily interactions. Over the years he has picked up functional Hindi and conversational English by dealing with traders, tourists and government workers, though he cannot read or write in any language due to his illiterate background. He can understand basic Hindi TV shows and pick up common English phrases, especially those related to his work, such: ‘sap’, ‘fermentation’ and ‘price’. His linguistic curiosity often drives him to learn new words through story‑telling sessions and folk songs.
skills_and_expertise: Aravind is an experienced toddy tapper with deep knowledge of palm sap extraction, fermentation and the seasonal timing required for quality yield. He can identify local plants and their medicinal properties, a skill passed down through generations. His imagination fuels creative storytelling, making him a popular figure during village gatherings. He also excels at organising small community events, improvising solutions (jugaad) when equipment fails, and communicating basic English with traders to negotiate prices.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Toddy tapping", "Palm sap fermentation", "Local plant identification", "Folk storytelling", "Community event coordination", "Improvisational problem solving (jugaad)", "Basic conversational English"]
hobbies_and_interests: Aravind loves immersing himself in the vibrant world of Karnataka’s folk arts. He spends evenings listening to Yakshagana performances and often joins the drum circle (chende) during village festivals. Storytelling is a passion; he likes to weave imaginative tales for children and neighbors. He enjoys playing cricket with friends on the open field, sketching the palm groves and village scenes in a notebook, and experimenting with different toddy recipes to improve taste. When he can, he explores nearby forests to discover new plants and their uses, and he regularly participates in card games at the local tea stall.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Listening to Yakshagana performances", "Playing drums (chende) at festivals", "Folk storytelling", "Playing cricket", "Sketching village and palm grove scenes", "Experimenting with toddy recipes", "Exploring forest for new plants", "Card games at the tea stall"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Aravind aims to upgrade his toddy‑tapping operation by setting up a small cooperative that can bring in better tools and negotiate fair prices. He wants to mentor younger villagers in efficient tapping methods and basic money‑management, hoping to secure a steadier income for his family. Though he cannot pursue formal studies, his biggest ambition is to see his children attend school, graduate, and break the cycle of poverty. He also dreams of gaining recognition as a folk storyteller, perhaps recording his narratives for a local cultural archive, which would give him a sense of prestige among his peers.
persona_clinical_summary: A 46-year-old male toddy tapper from rural Shimoga, Karnataka, currently married, with an illiterate education level. His first language is Kannada, and he has functional Hindi and English skills. He lives in a rural zone and is part of a community cooperative.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
  [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]
  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]

  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]

  [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
  [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]

  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]

  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Insurance Claim / TPA Form for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Kannada (code=kn, script=Kannada). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once (parking/ambulance or motor claim line). Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 46
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Tappers, Other
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Karnataka
- district: Shimoga
- first_language: Kannada
- clinical_persona_summary: A 46-year-old male toddy tapper from rural Shimoga, Karnataka, currently married, with an illiterate education level. His first language is Kannada, and he has functional Hindi and English skills. He lives in a rural zone and is part of a community cooperative.

Document format guide for Insurance Claim / TPA Form (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
TPA claim — Policy [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|25]] [[GENDER|Male]] Aadhaar [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Motor / RTA vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|PB10AB1234]] (exactly once).
PAN [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]] IFSC [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]] account [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|...]]

MANDATORY inline entities (16 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER — Insurance Policy Number e.g. [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
- AADHAAR_NUMBER — Aadhaar Number e.g. [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PAN_NUMBER — PAN Number e.g. [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
- BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER — Bank Account Number e.g. [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
- IFSC_CODE — IFSC Code e.g. [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
- BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER — Bank Routing Number e.g. [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
- CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER — Credit Card Number e.g. [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
- CVV — CVV e.g. [[CVV|123]]
- PIN — PIN e.g. [[PIN|4829]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
TPA claim — Policy [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Ramesh Kumar]] [[AGE|46]] [[GENDER|Male]] Aadhaar [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|234567890123]]
Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Shimoga District Hospital]] District [[DISTRICT|Shimoga]]
Motor / RTA vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|KA05AB1234]] (exactly once).
PAN [[PAN_NUMBER|KLMNO5678P]] IFSC [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0005678]] account [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|6050100123456789]]
Bank routing [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0005678]]
Credit card [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|5555555555554444]] CVV [[CVV|456]]
Debit card PIN [[PIN|1234]]
Contact [[PHONE_NUMBER|9880123456]]
ರೋಗಿಯು 46 ವರ್ಷದ ಪುರುಷ, ಗ್ರಾಮೀಣ ಶಿವಮೊಗ್ಗ, ಕರ್ನಾಟಕದ ಟೊಡ್ಡಿ ಟ್ಯಾಪರ್ ಆಗಿದ್ದಾರೆ. ಅವರು ಪ್ರಸ್ತುತ ವಿವಾಹಿತರಾಗಿದ್ದು, ಅನಕ್ಷರಸ್ಥ ಶಿಕ್ಷಣ ಮಟ್ಟವನ್ನು ಹೊಂದಿದ್ದಾರೆ. ಅವರು ತೀವ್ರವಾದ ಗ್ಯಾಸ್ಟ್ರೋಎಂಟರೈಟಿಸ್‌ನೊಂದಿಗೆ ಬಂದರು ಮತ್ತು IV ದ್ರವ ನಿರ್ವಹಣೆ ಮತ್ತು ವೀಕ್ಷಣೆಗಾಗಿ ದಾಖಲಾಗಿದ್ದರು. ತನಿಖೆಗಳು ಮಲದ ಸೂಕ್ಷ್ಮದರ್ಶಕ ಮತ್ತು ಸಂಪೂರ್ಣ ರಕ್ತ ಕಣಗಳ ಎಣಿಕೆಯನ್ನು ಒಳಗೊಂಡಿವೆ. ಚಿಕಿತ್ಸೆಯು ಮೌಖಿಕ ಪುನರ್ಜಲೀಕರಣ ಲವಣಗಳು, ಇಂಟ್ರಾವೆನಸ್ ಡೆಕ್ಸ್ಟ್ರೋಸ್ ಸಲೈನ್ ಮತ್ತು ಆಂಟಿಬಯೋಟಿಕ್‌ಗಳ ಕೋರ್ಸ್ ಅನ್ನು ಒಳಗೊಂಡಿತ್ತು. ರೋಗಿಯನ್ನು ಮೂರನೇ ದಿನದಂದು ಆಹಾರದ ಸಲಹೆಯೊಂದಿಗೆ ಬಿಡುಗಡೆ ಮಾಡಲಾಯಿತು. ಕ್ಲೈಮ್ ಅಡ್ಮಿಷನ್, ಔಷಧಿ ಮತ್ತು ಪ್ರಯೋಗಾಲಯ ಪರೀಕ್ಷೆಗಳಿಗೆ ಸಂಬಂಧಿಸಿದ ವೆಚ್ಚಗಳಿಗಾಗಿ ಆಗಿದೆ.
generated_text_en:
TPA claim — Policy [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Ramesh Kumar]] [[AGE|46]] [[GENDER|Male]] Aadhaar [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|234567890123]]
Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Shimoga District Hospital]] District [[DISTRICT|Shimoga]]
Motor / RTA vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|KA05AB1234]] (exactly once).
PAN [[PAN_NUMBER|KLMNO5678P]] IFSC [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0005678]] account [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|6050100123456789]]
Bank routing [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0005678]]
Credit card [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|5555555555554444]] CVV [[CVV|456]]
Debit card PIN [[PIN|1234]]
Contact [[PHONE_NUMBER|9880123456]]
Patient is a 46-year-old male toddy tapper from rural Shimoga, Karnataka, currently married, with an illiterate education level. He presented with acute gastroenteritis and was admitted for IV fluid management and observation. Investigations included stool microscopy and complete blood count. Treatment involved oral rehydration salts, intravenous dextrose saline, and a course of antibiotics. The patient was discharged on the third day with dietary advice. The claim is for expenses related to admission, medication, and laboratory tests.
judge_reasoning: Kannada clinical narrative correct; all TYPEs allow-listed; male/46/gastro fit; Latin tags expected; no length or script issues in prose.

professional_persona: Esther Mary is a diligent abrasive slitting machine operator in a coastal manufacturing unit, expertly setting up equipment, monitoring blade wear, and maintaining meticulous production logs, while their methodical planning and cooperative yet assertive nature helps drive consistent quality and team coordination.
linguistic_persona: Esther Mary is a native Tamil speaker; they read and write fluently in Tamil, possess functional English for workplace instructions, and can recognize a handful of Hindi words from occasional media exposure.
sports_persona: Esther Mary is an avid cricket fan who regularly watches matches, especially supporting the Chennai Super Kings, and enjoys occasional morning walks along the coastal promenade, though they rarely participate in organized sports.
arts_persona: Esther Mary engages with the arts by singing devotional hymns in the church choir, enjoying Tamil cinema featuring actors Vijay and Nayanthara, listening to A.R. Rahman’s melodies, and reading novels by author Sujatha, which together enrich their cultural outlook.
travel_persona: Esther Mary prefers short road trips to nearby coastal towns like Kanyakumari and historic sites such as the Mahabalipuram temples, often planning family outings with a modest budget, while dreaming of a future visit to Sri Lanka’s cultural heartland.
culinary_persona: Esther Mary delights in preparing traditional Tamil vegetarian dishes such as dosa, idli, sambar, rasam, and pongal, favoring ingredients like tamarind, curry leaves, and mustard seeds, and they often experiment with new spice blends for community feasts.
persona: Esther Mary, a 38‑year‑old abrasive‑slitting specialist, blends meticulous craftsmanship with a love for community choir, Tamil vegetarian cooking, and cricket, while juggling ambitious career goals, prudent finances, and a habit of over‑stocking spices in their kitchen.
cultural_background: Esther Mary is part of the Tamil Christian community in Thoothukkudi, a coastal town in Tamil Nadu. The region blends a traditional agrarian and fishing lifestyle with a modest industrial presence where she works. Her family observes Tamil cultural practices such as Pongal, respect for elders, and community gatherings, while also celebrating Christian festivals like Christmas, Easter, and Sunday worship. Growing up in a rural setting, she internalises the collective values of modesty, hard work, and hospitality, infused with the occasional exposure to modern manufacturing influences.
linguistic_background: Esther’s mother tongue is Tamil, which she reads, writes, and speaks fluently. She completed primary school in a Tamil‑medium government school, giving her functional literacy and basic numeracy. While she has limited formal training in English, she can understand simple workplace instructions in English and communicate essential information with her coworkers. She has no formal knowledge of Hindi, but occasional exposure to Hindi media in the region allows her to recognize a few common words.
skills_and_expertise: Esther is a proficient operator of abrasive slitting machines, adept at setting up the equipment, adjusting feed rates, and monitoring blade wear. She follows strict safety protocols, conducts routine inspections, and performs basic maintenance tasks such as lubrication and part replacement. Her meticulous attention to detail ensures high‑quality output with minimal defects. Additionally, she is skilled in record‑keeping, maintaining production logs, and coordinating with the quality control team to address any issues promptly.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Abrasive slitting machine operation", "Equipment setup and adjustment", "Feed rate optimization", "Blade wear monitoring", "Safety protocol adherence", "Routine equipment inspection", "Basic machine maintenance (lubrication, part replacement)", "Quality assurance coordination", "Production log maintenance", "Team communication and coordination"]
hobbies_and_interests: Esther enjoys singing in the church choir, cooking traditional Tamil vegetarian meals, tending a small kitchen garden, reading devotional books and Tamil novels, watching Tamil movies, taking part in community festivals and cultural events, casually watching cricket matches with friends, and doing DIY home improvement projects during her free time. Her sociable nature makes her a regular participant in group activities, and her curiosity drives her to explore new recipes and crafts.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Church choir singing", "Cooking traditional Tamil vegetarian dishes", "Gardening and home vegetable cultivation", "Reading devotional literature and Tamil novels", "Watching Tamil cinema", "Participating in community festivals and cultural events", "Casual cricket match viewing", "DIY home improvement projects"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Esther aims to deepen her technical knowledge by enrolling in short‑term training programmes on advanced abrasive slitting technology, safety management, and lean manufacturing. Over the next three to five years, she hopes to be promoted to senior operator or shift supervisor, leading a small team and driving process improvements. Simultaneously, she wishes to secure a stable financial future for her family, ensuring her children's higher education and eventually starting a modest home‑based tailoring or crafts business, leveraging her organizational skills and community network.
persona_clinical_summary: A 38‑year‑old female from Tamil Nadu, Thoothukkudi district, works as a primary‑educated abrasive‑slitting machine operator in a rural setting. She is married, speaks Tamil, and leads a community‑oriented lifestyle with modest financial planning. Her daily routine includes monitoring industrial equipment and engaging in community choir activities.

user_prompt:
Write one complete OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Tamil (code=ta, script=Tamil). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 38
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Slitting Machine Operator (Abrasive)
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Rural
- state: Tamil Nadu
- district: Thoothukkudi
- first_language: Tamil
- clinical_persona_summary: A 38‑year‑old female from Tamil Nadu, Thoothukkudi district, works as a primary‑educated abrasive‑slitting machine operator in a rural setting. She is married, speaks Tamil, and leads a community‑oriented lifestyle with modest financial planning. Her daily routine includes monitoring industrial equipment and engaging in community choir activities.

Document format guide for OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (adapt language/script; keep tags):
OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital X]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-X-01]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] | DOB [[DOB|1992-03-14]] | Age: [[AGE|34]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Female]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. ...]]
District: [[DISTRICT|...]] | Chief complaint: <short clinical line>

MANDATORY inline entities (13 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- OCCUPATION — Occupation e.g. [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- EMPLOYEE_ID — Employee ID e.g. [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
OPD சீட்டு | தோத்துகுகுடி மாவட்ட மருத்துவமனை | அடையாள எண் [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-TN-027]]
நோயாளி: [[PATIENT_NAME|Lakshmi Devi]] | பிறந்த தேதி [[DOB|1986-08-22]] | வயது: [[AGE|38]] | பாலினம்: [[GENDER|Female]]
தொழில்: [[OCCUPATION|Slitting Machine Operator (Abrasive)]] | மருத்துவப் பதிவு எண்: [[MRN|OPD-2024-0815-002]] | மருத்துவர்: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. K. Ananthan]]
மாவட்டம்: [[DISTRICT|Thoothukkudi]] | முதன்மைப் புகார்: கடந்த 3 வாரங்களாகத் தொடரும் இருமல் மற்றும் குறைந்த அளவிலான காய்ச்சல்.
தற்போதைய நோயின் வரலாறு: நோயாளிக்கு மஞ்சள் நிறச் சளியுடன் கூடிய இருமல், கடந்த மூன்று வாரங்களாக மாலை நேரங்களில் குறைந்த அளவிலான காய்ச்சல் இருப்பதாகத் தெரிவிக்கிறார். மேலும், லேசான இரவு நேர வியர்வை மற்றும் 2 கிலோ உடல் எடை குறைவு ஆகியவையும் உள்ளன. இரத்தத்துடன் சளி வெளியேறுவதை (Hemoptysis) அவர் மறுக்கிறார். மருந்துக் கடைகளில் வாங்கும் இருமல் மருந்துகள் அவரது அறிகுறிகளில் எந்த மாற்றத்தையும் ஏற்படுத்தவில்லை.
உடல் பரிசோதனை:
- பொதுவான நிலை: விழிப்புணர்வுடன், நடுத்தர உடல்வாகு, நாள்பட்ட நோயால் பாதிக்கப்பட்டவர் போலத் தெரிகிறார்.
- முக்கிய அறிகுறிகள்: இரத்த அழுத்தம் 120/80 mmHg, இதயத் துடிப்பு நிமிடத்திற்கு 88, சுவாச வீதம் நிமிடத்திற்கு 18, உடல் வெப்பநிலை 37.8°C.
- சுவாச மண்டலம்: மார்புப் பரிசோதனையில் வலது மேல் பகுதியில் சுவாச ஒலிகள் குறைந்து காணப்படுகின்றன, அவ்வப்போது மெல்லிய சலசலப்பு ஒலிகள் கேட்கின்றன. மூச்சிரைப்பு அல்லது மூச்சுச் சத்தம் ஏதும் கேட்கவில்லை.
ஆய்வுகள்:
- AFB பரிசோதனைக்கான சளி: நேர்மறை (2+).
- மார்பு எக்ஸ்-ரே: வலது மேல் மடலில் 2.5 செ.மீ. அளவுள்ள குழி போன்ற புண்ணுடன் (Cavitary lesion) சுற்றியுள்ள பகுதிகளில் ஊடுருவல்கள் (Infiltrates) காணப்படுகின்றன.
நோய் கண்டறிதல்: நுரையீரல் காசநோய், வலது மேல் மடல்.
சிகிச்சைத் திட்டம்:
- வகை I DOTS சிகிச்சை முறை தொடங்கப்பட்டது: ஐசோனியாசிட், ரிஃபாம்பின், பைராசினமைடு, எத்தம்புட்டால்.
- மருந்து உட்கொள்வதில் ஒழுக்கம் மற்றும் முழுமையான 6 மாத சிகிச்சையை முடிப்பதன் அவசியம் குறித்து நோயாளிக்கு ஆலோசனை வழங்கப்பட்டது.
- சளிப் பரிசோதனை மற்றும் கண்காணிப்பிற்காக 2 வாரங்களில் மீண்டும் வர அறிவுறுத்தப்பட்டது.
- மேலாண்மைக்காக மாவட்ட காசநோய் மையத்திற்குப் பரிந்துரைக்கப்பட்டது.
உறவினர் தொடர்பு: [[RELATIVE_NAME|R. Murugan]] | தொலைபேசி: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9445678901]]
பணியாளர் அடையாள எண்: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-TN-4589]]
generated_text_en:
OPD Slip | District Hospital Thoothukkudi | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-TN-027]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|Lakshmi Devi]] | DOB [[DOB|1986-08-22]] | Age: [[AGE|38]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Female]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Slitting Machine Operator (Abrasive)]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-0815-002]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. K. Ananthan]]
District: [[DISTRICT|Thoothukkudi]] | Chief complaint: Persistent cough for 3 weeks with low-grade fever.
History of Present Illness: Patient reports a productive cough, sometimes with yellowish sputum, and a low-grade evening fever for the past three weeks. She also experiences mild night sweats and a 2 kg weight loss. She denies any hemoptysis. Her symptoms are not relieved by over-the-counter cough syrups.
Physical Examination:
- General: Alert, moderately built, appears chronically ill.
- Vital Signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, Pulse 88/min, Respiratory Rate 18/min, Temperature 37.8°C.
- Respiratory: Chest examination reveals decreased breath sounds in the right upper zone with occasional fine crackles. No wheezing or rhonchi heard.
Investigations:
- Sputum for AFB: Positive (2+).
- Chest X-ray: Shows a 2.5 cm cavitary lesion in the right upper lobe with surrounding infiltrates.
Diagnosis: Pulmonary Tuberculosis, Right Upper Lobe.
Treatment Plan:
- Initiated on Category I DOTS regimen: Isoniazid, Rifampicin, Pyrazinamide, Ethambutol.
- Patient counseled on medication adherence and importance of completing the full 6-month course.
- Follow-up scheduled in 2 weeks for sputum re-check and monitoring.
- Referral to District TB Center for further management.
Relative Contact: [[RELATIVE_NAME|R. Murugan]] | Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9445678901]]
Employee ID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-TN-4589]]
judge_reasoning: Tamil prose with valid allow-listed entities; no script drift, persona mismatch, or length issues.
